name: Snapshot-Based Planning Review and Executive Summary Workflow

top_nav_changes.legacy_executive_print_behavior: Internal executive_print mode redirects to Planning Snapshot > Executive Summary for compatibility.

snapshot_object_design.storage: frontend localStorage plus dashboard provider state
snapshot_object_design.phase22a_limitation: Snapshot creation is selected-parcel focused in this phase; map/camera extent is described but not persisted as a formal GIS bookmark.

overview_snapshot_button.visible_when: A parcel is selected in Overview
overview_snapshot_button.empty_guidance: When no parcel is selected, Overview instructs users to search/click a parcel and then save a Planning Snapshot.

empty_state.title: Create a Planning Snapshot
empty_state.message: Use Overview to choose a parcel, map context, and layers, then save a snapshot for review and reporting.

executive_summary_generation.title: Planning Snapshot Executive Summary
executive_summary_generation.print_behavior: window.print() after switching to the Executive Summary view
executive_summary_generation.print_css: Existing print-report/no-print styles are reused for light/white report output and hidden app chrome.

documentation_updates: README current product modes updated to Overview / Planning Snapshot / Methodology
demo walkthrough updated to save a Planning Snapshot before opening Executive Summary
final demo checklist updated for Save Snapshot for Report and Planning Snapshot
2-minute demo script updated to explain saved snapshots and explainable metrics
higher-up speaking notes updated to use Planning Snapshot and Executive Summary wording

safety_verification.ui_result: No parcel-level prediction probability or ranking-class terms were found in src/components.
safety_verification.readme_note: README still documents internal ranking class names as historical/model governance artifacts; these are not exposed in the UI workflow.

validation_results.npm_run_build_webpack: passed on rerun; first attempt hit command timeout, second completed successfully
validation_results.backend_compile_or_pytest: not run; backend files were not changed

runtime_smoke_results.dev_server_action: Existing CFS dev listeners on ports 3000 and 8000 were stale; restarted only CFS-owned node/uvicorn listeners and ran npm run dev:cfs.

browser_smoke.caveat: The in-app browser remained in compact-width layout, hiding the top parcel search. Browser verification covered navigation, empty state, Methodology, and safety terms; parcel search/detail availability was verified through backend APIs.

remaining_known_issues: Phase 22A snapshot storage is local/browser-only; multi-user persistence would require a future backend design.
Map extent/camera capture is described but not serialized as a formal GIS bookmark in this phase.
The in-app browser compact viewport prevented full click-through testing of top-search parcel selection and Save Snapshot for Report, though typecheck/lint/build and backend API smoke passed.
recommended_next_step: Phase 22B should add a small snapshot manager/history and formal map-view bookmark capture if the localStorage workflow feels right in demo.
